Container.Name: OpenClaw-Mission-Control
Container.Overview: OpenClaw Mission Control is an AI agent orchestration platform. This single-container edition bundles PostgreSQL, Redis, the backend API, the webhook worker, and the Next.js frontend — no external services required. Two host ports must be mapped: the frontend UI (default 3000) and the backend API (default 8000). The browser calls the backend API port directly for authentication and all API requests, so both ports must be reachable from your browser. PostgreSQL and Redis run on loopback inside the container and do not need host port mappings. Set BASE_URL, CORS_ORIGINS, and NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL to your Unraid server's real IP address (not localhost) so that browser requests are routed and accepted correctly.

Config.Name: Frontend UI Port
Config.Description: Host port mapped to the Next.js frontend (browser UI). This is the port you open in your browser.

Config.Name: Backend API Port
Config.Description: Host port mapped to the FastAPI backend (REST API). The browser calls this port directly for all API requests including token validation — it must be reachable from your browser, not just internally. If you change this from the default 8000, update NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and BASE_URL accordingly.

Config.Name: App Data
Config.Description: Persistent storage for PostgreSQL data, Redis snapshots, and application configuration.

Config.Name: AUTH_MODE
Config.Description: Authentication mode. Use 'local' for token-based auth or 'clerk' for Clerk JWT auth.

Config.Name: LOCAL_AUTH_TOKEN
Config.Description: Required when AUTH_MODE=local. Must be at least 50 characters. Use a strong random value.

Config.Name: BASE_URL
Config.Description: Public URL of the backend, used in generated gateway instructions and webhook payloads. Must be reachable from outside the container. Replace 'localhost' with your Unraid server's IP or hostname (e.g. http://192.168.1.10:8000). If you mapped the backend to a different host port, use that port here.

Config.Name: CORS_ORIGINS
Config.Description: Comma-separated list of browser origins the backend will accept requests from. Must match exactly how you access the frontend in your browser. Replace 'localhost' with your Unraid server's IP or hostname (e.g. http://192.168.1.10:3000). Using 'localhost' here while accessing via an IP address will cause 'Unable to reach backend' errors.

Config.Name: NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL
Config.Description: URL the browser uses to reach the backend API. 'auto' derives the URL from the browser's current hostname and assumes the backend is on port 8000 — this works correctly when you access Unraid by its real IP or hostname. Set an explicit URL (e.g. http://192.168.1.10:8000) if you changed the backend host port or are accessing via a reverse proxy.

Config.Name: LOG_LEVEL
Config.Description: Backend log level. One of: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR.
